title: Solid Electrolyzer Demand Drives Plug Power: Will the Momentum Last?
text: Solid electrolyzer demand is driving Plug Power's growth, with Q2 2025 revenues from the electrolyzer product line surging approximately 200% on a year-over-year basis. Electrolyzer products constituted 26% of PLUG's total business. Plug Power secured an order to install a total of 10 arrays of GenEco electrolyzers with Hydrogen Processing Units by early 2026. In October 2025, Plug Power signed a deal with Allied Biofuels to deliver a maximum of 2 GW of GenEco PEM electrolyzer systems, bringing its total contracted electrolyzer capacity with Allied partners to 5 GW. Additionally, Plug Power was awarded a major Front-End Engineering Design contract for a large PEM electrolyzer system in Quebec that supports Hy2gen's Courant project. Strong electrolyzer demand positions Plug Power well for stronger revenue growth in the quarters ahead. However, service revenues are expected to decline in Q1 and the electrolyzer backlog remains at a multi-year low. Large-scale hydrogen and e-fuel projects are expected to move to final investment decisions.
